name: Facebook
manualSteps: Log in to your Facebook account
Go to Settings & Privacy → Settings → Your Facebook Information
Click 'Deactivation and Deletion'
Select 'Delete Account' and click 'Continue to Account Deletion'
Enter your password and click 'Continue'
Click 'Delete Account' — your account enters a 30-day grace period
Do NOT log in during the 30-day period or deletion is cancelled
notes: Meta defaults to 'Deactivate' instead of 'Delete' — users must explicitly choose permanent deletion. 30-day grace period; logging in cancels deletion. Full data purge may take up to 90 days after the grace period.

name: Instagram
manualSteps: Log in to your Instagram account on the web
Visit the account deletion page directly (link provided)
Select a reason for leaving from the dropdown
Re-enter your password
Click 'Delete [username]'
Account enters 30-day grace period before permanent deletion
Do NOT log in during the 30-day period or deletion is cancelled
notes: Owned by Meta. Same 30-day grace period as Facebook. Deletion page is not linked from settings — you must navigate to it directly. Content on Threads (if linked) is not automatically deleted.

name: X / Twitter
manualSteps: Log in to your X account
Go to Settings and Support → Settings and Privacy → Your Account
Click 'Deactivate your account'
Read the information and click 'Deactivate'
Enter your password to confirm
Account is deactivated immediately and permanently deleted after 30 days
Do NOT log in during the 30-day period or deactivation is reversed
notes: Formerly Twitter. Deactivation is the first step — permanent deletion happens automatically after 30 days. Username becomes available to others after deletion.

name: TikTok
manualSteps: Open TikTok and go to your Profile
Tap the menu icon (☰) then Settings and Privacy
Tap 'Manage Account'
Tap 'Delete Account' at the bottom
Follow the verification prompts
Account is deactivated for 30 days, then permanently deleted
You can recover the account by logging in within 30 days
notes: Deletion is easiest through the mobile app. Web settings have limited account management. 30-day grace period applies.

name: LinkedIn
manualSteps: Log in to your LinkedIn account
Click your profile icon → Settings & Privacy
Under Account preferences, click 'Account management'
Click 'Close account'
Select a reason for closing and click 'Next'
Re-enter your password and click 'Close Account'
Account closure is processed within 24 hours
notes: LinkedIn aggressively tries to retain users through the closure flow with multiple confirmation screens. Owned by Microsoft. Premium subscribers must cancel subscription first. Data may be retained for up to 30 days after closure.

name: Reddit
manualSteps: Log in to your Reddit account
Go to User Settings → Account
Scroll to the bottom and click 'Delete Account'
Check the box confirming you understand the consequences
Enter your username and password
Click 'Delete'
notes: Account deletion is immediate and irreversible. Posts and comments remain on the platform but are disassociated from your username (shown as [deleted]). To remove content, manually delete posts/comments before deleting the account.

name: Snapchat
manualSteps: Visit the Snapchat account deletion page (link provided)
Log in with your Snapchat credentials
Your account will be deactivated immediately
After 30 days of deactivation, the account is permanently deleted
During the 30-day period, friends cannot contact or interact with you
Logging back in during this period reactivates the account
notes: Snapchat calls it 'deactivation' but it leads to permanent deletion after 30 days. The deletion portal is a dedicated page separate from the app settings.

name: Pinterest
manualSteps: Log in to your Pinterest account
Go to Settings → Account management
Click 'Delete your data and account'
Select a reason and click 'Continue'
Click 'Send email' to receive a confirmation link
Click the link in the confirmation email within 14 days
Account will be permanently deleted after a 14-day grace period
notes: Pinterest requires email confirmation to initiate deletion. If you don't click the confirmation link within 14 days, the request expires. Account can be reactivated during the 14-day grace period by logging in.

name: Tumblr
manualSteps: Log in to your Tumblr account
Go to Settings → Account
Click 'Delete Account' at the bottom of the page
Enter your email address and password to confirm
Click 'Delete Everything'
Account and all content are permanently deleted
notes: Owned by Automattic (WordPress parent company). Deletion is straightforward and relatively fast. Individual blogs can be deleted separately without deleting the entire account.

name: Discord
manualSteps: Open Discord and go to User Settings (gear icon)
Click 'My Account'
Scroll down and click 'Delete Account'
Enter your password (and 2FA code if enabled)
Click 'Delete Account' to confirm
Account is scheduled for deletion within 14-30 days
notes: If you own any servers, you must transfer ownership or delete them before you can delete your account. Messages you've sent in servers will remain but show a 'Deleted User' tag. DMs are deleted.
